items.headline: City Reports on Youth Services and Library Facility Plans
items.summary: The City Council received status updates on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan and the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports outline the city's current progress and future direction for youth programs and library services.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning decision for 333 Franklin Street has been delayed with no new hearing date set yet. Residents waiting to learn about this project's outcome should watch for a new notice from the city.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This ongoing infrastructure project addresses traffic and safety coordination between vehicle and rail traffic on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: Cupertino updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council reviewed Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, which guides how Cupertino prioritizes biking and walking infrastructure. The plan's scoring system was revised, which will affect which neighborhood projects get funded and built next.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract for unnamed project
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution to approve project plans, specifications, and award a construction contract, though the agenda does not specify which project or construction work is involved. Residents should check meeting minutes for details on what infrastructure or facilities are being built.

items.headline: Milpitas updates federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council considered a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 budget, which governs how federal housing and community development funds are allocated. This could affect affordable housing initiatives and community programs in Milpitas.

items.headline: New 65-unit senior housing complex approved for East St John
items.summary: Trillium, a nearly $8.8M senior apartment building with 65 units, is coming to 678 E St John St. This represents significant new housing for San José's aging population during an ongoing housing shortage.

items.headline: City Council Approves Emergency Interim Housing Siting Policy
items.summary: San José City Council approved a new emergency interim housing siting policy, which will affect where temporary housing facilities can be located throughout the city. This policy change could influence homeless services expansion and housing availability in residential neighborhoods.
